channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: South African research on dog fights in homes and practical prevention tips
item.description: A new study by veterinary scientists from the University of Pretoria in South Africa has found that most dog fights and bite wounds among dogs happen inside people’s homes rather than on the streets. ...

item.title: Neon Studio Aims for Seventh Consecutive Palme d'Or at Cannes Festival
item.description: Neon, a small independent film distributor founded in 2017, has achieved an unprecedented streak at the Cannes Film Festival, winning the Palme d'Or six years in a row. Led by co-founder Tom Quinn, Ne...

item.title: AFP and PNP to boost security for BARMM’s inaugural parliamentary election
item.description: The Western Mindanao Command (Westmincom) announced that additional military forces will be deployed to the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) to ensure a secure environment for i...

item.title: Remembering Michael Pennington: A Versatile Actor and Scholar of Theatre
item.description: Michael Pennington, who has died aged 82, was celebrated for his extraordinary versatility as an actor, his insightful writing, and his engaging wit. A stalwart of the Royal Shakespeare Company during...

item.title: Former West Bengal Minister Sujit Bose Arrested by ED Over Municipality Recruitment Scam
item.description: In a significant development, the Enforcement Directorate (ED) arrested former West Bengal minister and Trinamool Congress leader Sujit Bose on Monday in connection with the municipality recruitment s...

item.title: Former Manitoba hockey coach sentenced to six years for sexual abuse of teenage player
item.description: Madison Biluk, a 31-year-old former hockey coach from Manitoba, has begun serving a six-year prison sentence after pleading guilty to luring and sexually assaulting a teenage player on her team. The o...

item.title: Severe Berg winds bring damage to property, vehicles and injuries across KwaZulu-Natal
item.description: Severe Berg wind conditions swept through KwaZulu-Natal on Sunday, with gusts reaching up to 116km/h, causing significant damage to property and infrastructure. The South African Weather Service issue...

item.title: UK Government to Nationalise British Steel in Public Ownership Move
item.description: Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has announced that the UK Government will bring British Steel into full public ownership. Legislation will be introduced this week to grant the necessary powers, subjec...

item.title: Albie Casiño and LA Santos Join Forces Against Lou Yanong and River Joseph on Kapamilya Chat
item.description: ABS-CBN’s popular online show, Kapamilya Chat, recently featured an exciting and entertaining game segment where well-loved stars Albie Casiño and LA Santos joined forces to compete against Lou Yanong...

item.title: Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool Renovation Costs Rise Sharply Amid Controversy Over No-Bid Contract
item.description: The renovation of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool in Washington, D.C., has surged in cost from an initial estimate of $1.8 million to $13.1 million. The no-bid contract was awarded to Virginia-ba...

item.title: HEC launches fully online and digital degree attestation system
item.description: The Higher Education Commission (HEC) of Pakistan has announced a major upgrade to its Degree Attestation System (DAS), now offering a fully online and paperless process for students and graduates. HE...

item.title: Families and rights groups commemorate 'Negros 19' and call for justice
item.description: On May 12, 2026, families of the victims and human rights advocates gathered to pay tribute to the 'Negros 19', marking another year of remembrance for the tragic incident in Negros Occidental. The ev...

item.title: Afrika Bambaataa, Influential Hip Hop Figure, Passes Away at 68
item.description: Afrika Bambaataa, a seminal figure in the development of hip hop culture and founder of the Universal Zulu Nation, has passed away at the age of 68. Born in New York City in 1957, Bambaataa was instru...

item.title: Teaching assistant imprisoned for physically abusing pupils
item.description: A teaching assistant has been sentenced to prison after admitting to multiple instances of child cruelty. The individual was found to have physically abused pupils by pinching and spitting at them, ac...

item.title: Chris Hedges on America's Internal Decay and the Rise of Oligarchic Rule Under Trump
item.description: In this opinion piece, author Chris Hedges argues that the United States is collapsing from within due to moral, social, and spiritual decay rather than external threats. He traces the roots of this d...

item.title: Lamu residents face rising danger from unstable sea transport routes
item.description: Lamu’s sea transport system has become increasingly unsafe, with residents relying heavily on waterways that are often affected by rough tides, overcrowding, poor coordination and weak enforcement of ...

item.title: Understanding the Unseasonal Warmth and Dryness of Autumn in Australia
item.description: Australia experienced an unusually warm and dry April, with many regions seeing above-average temperatures and minimal rainfall. New South Wales recorded its second-driest April on record, and Bairnsd...

item.title: Health Canada Issues Warning on Infant Self-Feeding Devices Due to Safety Risks
item.description: Health Canada has issued a consumer warning against infant self-feeding devices that were previously available for purchase on Amazon Canada, citing significant choking and aspiration hazards. These p...

item.title: Supreme Court Urges High Courts to Form Committees for Resolving Bar-Bench Disputes
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has issued directions to all high courts to consider forming grievance redressal committees at high court, district, and taluka levels. This move comes in the wake of a cont...

item.title: Research Highlights Fructose's Role in Metabolic Health and Disease
item.description: A recent review from the University of Colorado Anschutz, published in Nature Metabolism, indicates that fructose, a common sugar found in table sugar and high-fructose corn syrup, has unique effects ...
